meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve SaaS Knowledge Gaps
content.seo_description: Bridge your SaaS knowledge gap with this expert analytics checklist. Learn to implement tracking using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver client value.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing and start measuring. To bridge your SaaS knowledge gap, implement a lightweight event-tracking layer immediately. By integrating PostHog or Mixpanel via Antigravity, you capture critical user behavior data. Use Claude Code to automate the instrumentation, ensuring every feature launch provides the actionable insights your clients demand.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect interface, but the moment the client asks for conversion metrics, the silence is deafening. Operating without data isn't just a technical oversight; it’s a career risk that leaves you vulnerable to scope creep and client churn. You are building in the dark, and your reputation is on the line.

But the fog is lifting. By standardizing your analytics infrastructure, you transform from a 'coder for hire' into a data-driven partner who drives business outcomes. This blueprint uses Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, giving you enterprise-grade observability in minutes, not days.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that define success for your client's SaaS, such as 'signup_completed', 'feature_activated', and 'subscription_upgraded'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead SaaS Architect. Create a schema for tracking 'signup_completed', 'feature_activated', and 'subscription_upgraded' events using a standard analytics payload format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking-schema.json file based on the identified core business metrics.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate your chosen SDK (PostHog is recommended for freelancers) into your main application wrapper to ensure global availability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable React hook 'useAnalytics' that initializes PostHog and provides a trackEvent method with automatic user identification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject the useAnalytics hook into the App.tsx root component and configure the provider.'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Rather than manually adding tracking to every button, use a centralized event handler to capture interactions efficiently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that wraps standard form submissions and CTA clicks to automatically trigger analytics events without polluting business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor all primary CTA buttons to include the trackEvent utility function.'

steps.title: Implement User Property Mapping
steps.description: Associate events with user profiles to see the 'who' behind the 'what', critical for client reporting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function to sync user metadata like 'plan_type' and 'signup_date' to the analytics platform upon successful authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the auth-service to call the syncUserProperties function on login.'

steps.title: Validate and Debug Data Flow
steps.description: Ensure your events are firing correctly in the staging environment before handing the project over to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a debug script that logs all outgoing analytics payloads to the console during development to verify event structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Enable verbose analytics logging in the development environment and verify the event stream.'

pro_tips: Always namespace your events (e.g., 'ui:button_click') to keep your dashboard clean as the project grows.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your events; stick to anonymous IDs to stay GDPR compliant.
Set up 'Autocapture' in PostHog initially, then manually instrument only the 'money' events to save time.
Create a custom dashboard for the client within 24 hours of launch; it makes you look like a pro immediately.
Use a 'staging' analytics project key to avoid polluting your production data with test events during development.
content.next_steps: Once the events are flowing, schedule a 15-minute walkthrough with your client to show them their own data, establishing your role as a strategic technical partner.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical edge to turn raw code into high-value business insights. Don't just build features—build success metrics that keep clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
